title: Ep 77 Why Is My Dog Barking at the TV? (And What You Can Do About It) & Too Close for Comfort
podcast_name: Help! My Dog: The Podcast. Dog Behaviour & Training Strategies that Work!
summary: Have you ever felt like your dog has a personal vendetta against the TV? You’re not alone! In this episode, we’re diving into a listener's question about a dog who just can’t stop barking at animals on the screen. It’s a surprisingly common problem with surprisingly few answers online, and it often leaves owners feeling stuck and overwhelmed (possibly with broken TVs!). Why is your dog reacting like that to the latest Attenborough documentary as if elephants and giraffes just invaded your living room? And more importantly, what can you actually do about it? Tune in as we unpack the hidden emotions behind TV reactivity and how you can begin to shift your dog’s response. ................ Whether your dog is unpredictable with other dogs or predictably bad… Whether it looks like freezing, barking, lunging or running away… Whether you are just starting out on your dog-owning journey or your dog has been labelled as having a “behaviour problem”… … this episode is for you and is going to give you huge insights! Want to join the Help! My Dog Online HUB? Here's the link: www.helpmydog.com/hub Dr Tom Mitchell is a world-renowned veterinarian, behaviourist and dog trainer and director of the Behavet behaviour clinic (rated Excellent on Trustpilot), which sees over 400 pet behaviour consultations each and every month no matter where you are in the world. In 2023, he founded Help! My Dog, an online dog behaviour learning platform to help even more owners all over the world and show that dog behaviour transformation is not only possible but it's FUN, with the right know-how! Make sure to subscribe/follow the podcast for the free weekly episodes! Useful Links: Follow Help! My Dog on Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/helpmydogofficial/ Schedule a Consultation with Behavet: https://www.beha.vet/ Follow Behavet on Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/behavet

title: 101 | What If You’re Scared of Your Dog?
podcast_name: Beyond Obedience | Dog Training, Dog Behaviour Training, Scared Dogs, Reactive Dogs
summary: Hey Dog Lover,🐾 Have you ever felt that gut punch of fear when your dog lunges, growls, or just gets a little too intense? This episode is for you.  I’m pulling back the curtain on a real moment with my horse Sunny—and sharing how fear, uncertainty, and doubt (aka the FUD Factor) can derail our leadership in a heartbeat. We’ll unpack: Why fear is a leadership killer—and what to do about it How your nervous system is your training strategy Why responding > reacting matters more than any obedience cue This is a vulnerable, must-listen episode if you’ve ever thought, “I love my dog, but I’m scared of how they behave.”  If you ever doubted your ability to handle an off-leash dog. If you have ever wondered: "Do I have what it takes?" Hope you enjoy this one. 💜 Remember to comment, share, and leave a ⭐️5-star rating!  It will help more overwhelmed dog lovers find their way back to calm. Next Steps:Join the Fearless Framework course + community.  It’s where you’ll learn to lead with calm confidence, ditch outdated training methods, and finally feel like you and your dog are on the same team again. 🐾 Can't wait to share this journey with you!  Music credit: Shawn Trotter – www.shawntrotter.com

title: Ep. 10. Dog Growling at Baby? Understanding Toddler Behavior, Body Language & Preventing Bites
podcast_name: Dog and Baby Podcast: Dog training tips for expecting parents
summary: If your dog has been acting different since your baby started crawling or if you're constantly on edge wondering if things are about to go sideways this episode is for you. We’re talking I'm answering a listener question that I believe many parents can relate to. From growling at the food bowl to pacing around the baby, I’m breaking down exactly why this happens, what your dog is trying to communicate, and how toddler development plays a major role in dog behavior. This is not just about training your dog, it’s about training yourself to understand exactly what's happening and act fast, protect, and guide everyone in your home. Without punishment. Without guilt. And without guessing. 👇 Here’s what we cover: — The truth about toddler sensory behavior & how it triggers your dog— Why growling is not a bad thing— What resource guarding actually looks like (and how to handle it)— The #1 mistake parents make with toddlers and dogs— My exact step-by-step training plan to restore calm and create safety Whether you’re already in the thick of it or trying to prep before baby arrives, you’ll walk away from this episode feeling clear, supported, and ready to lead. ✨ Book a 1:1 Dog + Baby Strategy Session:I’m now offering limited spots for 1:1 online private session to help you create a safer, more structured plan for life with your dog and baby. These are fully personalized and rooted in real-life, not one-size-fits-all advice.👉 Book your session here(Spots are limited...I only take a 4 per month!) 🐾 Free Gift: Dog + Baby Prep ChecklistWant a free checklist to help you set your dog up for success before baby even arrives?Message me the word “guide” on my latest post at @woofandwomb and I’ll DM it to you! 📖 Resource Mentioned: Dog Body Language BookIf you want to get better at reading your dog before things escalate, this is the book I always recommend:👉 Doggie Language by Lili Chin

title: Are R+ Trainers Frauds? Can A Dog Come When Called Around Wildlife Without Using Punishers? #305
podcast_name: Shaped by Dog with Susan Garrett
summary: Visit us at shapedbydog.com      Can you really stop a dog from chasing deer or anything else without an e-collar? Yes, and I’m sharing how. There’s an ongoing commentary that it's impossible to train certain dogs, especially those with high prey drive, to come when called using only reinforcement-based methods. Now, those of us who are reinforcement-based trainers know that it's absolutely possible. The secret lies in understanding the Canine Predatory Motor Sequence. Rather than trying to stop dogs at the end of the sequence when it's way too late, we focus on the early stages and what we want our dogs to do.      In this episode, you’ll hear:     • Why some say reinforcement-based training alone can’t stop high prey drive dogs.  • How the canine predatory motor sequence works and why it matters for your dog’s behavior  • Why suppressing behavior isn’t training, and what I do instead.  • The three common ways people deal with behavior problems and which one really works.  • How I use predatory redirection to interrupt chase drive.  • A real-life example of Prophet ignoring deer just 15 feet away.  • How we changed Tater Salad’s behavior using relationship-building games.  • Special opportunity to join my Home School the Dog program.  • Why head halters can act like emergency brakes when you need them.  • How I redirect Kim’s rescue dog Waffles from distractions to focus back to me and what you can take away from it.     Join Home School the Dog:  To join Home School the Dog at a special discount, write to us at wag@dogsthat.com with the subject line: Prey Drive    Learn To Play ItsYerChoice:  Learn how to play the ItsYerChoice (IYC) Game - https://recallers.com/iycsummit-join/    Hand Target and More Foundational Games  Get the Hand Target Game and more foundational training games in our free Connected Dog Season of Nuance Video Series running for a limited time - https://learn.doggyflix.com/     Resources:     1. title: Ep. 144 | WINTER FEEDING TIPS - What Your Horse Really Needs
summary: In this episode, Bryan and Peter dive into the hot topic of winter horse feeding.  They unpack smart strategies to maintain healthy body condition through the colder months while supporting every body system.  Is rugging the only thing you can do to keep your horse warm?  Does grain generate more internal heat than fibre?  Find out what really matters when feeding in winter and bust some common myths along the way. Campbells Wine

title: The Dopamine Secret: How to Make Your Dog Choose You Over the World
podcast_name: Listen Dog Training: The Walk & Talk
summary: Ever wondered why your dog seems more interested in a leaf blowing down the street than your recall cue? Or why their ears perk up at the sound of the fridge but not your voice? It all comes down to one powerful brain chemical: dopamine. In this episode of The Walk & Talk, I’m diving deep into the science of dopamine and how you can use it to massively boost your training results, strengthen your bond, and finally become more reinforcing than literally everything else in your dog’s world. Whether you’re working on recall, loose lead walking, or just want to be your dog’s favourite thing again—this episode is packed with insight and practical tweaks you can start using today.   In this episode, you’ll learn: 💡What dopamine actually is—and how it affects your dog’s behaviour 💡Why anticipation is just as powerful as the reward itself 💡How to make your training more engaging and brain-boosting 💡Simple ways to create dopamine spikes using rewards, patterns and movement 💡How to use surprise, variety and play to make YOU the most exciting part of your dog’s day 💡Real-life examples of tweaks that create faster, more reliable results   💻 Want to dive deeper into transforming your dog’s behaviour with science-backed strategies that actually work? Check out my digital training products, freebies and online courses at www.listendogtraining.com You can use code PODCAST10 for 10% off all my digital dog training packages, guides & courses!   📱 Let’s connect! I’d love to hear how you’re using the dopamine trick in your own training:  Follow me on Instagram and TikTok → @listendogtraining 🎧 If this episode gave you an “aha!” moment, I’d be so grateful if you left a review 🌟 or shared the episode with a fellow dog lover—it helps the podcast grow and helps more people train with confidence, clarity, and joy!

title: 100 | A Trail of Trust- 100 Episodes~ Where we were, Where we are going
podcast_name: Beyond Obedience | Dog Training, Dog Behaviour Training, Scared Dogs, Reactive Dogs
summary: Hey Dog Lover! In this very special 100th episode, Paisley and I are reflecting on the journey—how this podcast went from a final message to a full-blown mission. We’re celebrating the episodes you loved most, sharing powerful stories from our incredible community, and giving you a peek behind the scenes at what’s next for the Fearless Framework. If you’ve ever felt like you're walking through the world with a misunderstood dog and just want to get it right—this episode is for you. Here’s what we dig into: The top 5 most-downloaded episodes and why they resonated Real-life testimonials from fellow dog lovers (get your tissues ready) Why the Dogonality Quiz is such a game-changer How we’re upleveling the Fearless Framework and building a movement rooted in leadership, calm, and connection And yep… that one time I got kicked in the face 🙈 This isn’t just about training—it’s about trust, relationship, and learning to be the person your dog needs. title: Ep 76 The Smart Way to Train in a Multi-Dog Household & Woody’s Song
podcast_name: Help! My Dog: The Podcast. Dog Behaviour & Training Strategies that Work!
summary: Managing a multi-dog household can feel like juggling with one hand tied behind your back, especially when it comes to training. How does it even work and what are the steps? You’ve probably heard that working with your dogs one at a time is the way to go, but what does that actually look like in real life? What if your dogs struggle being apart, or one starts barking the moment the other gets your attention? This episode breaks down a common roadblock many dog owners face and explores how to make solo training not only doable but enjoyable and smooth. Dr Tom Mitchell, dog trainer and vet behaviourist, breaks things down step-by-step with practical ideas that can fit into your daily routine. ................ Whether your dog is unpredictable with other dogs or predictably bad… Whether it looks like freezing, barking, lunging or running away… Whether you are just starting out on your dog-owning journey or your dog has been labelled as having a “behaviour problem”… … this episode is for you and is going to give you huge insights! title: Training Holidays & Spicy Dogs: Adam Daines Brings the Fun
podcast_name: Positively Dog Training - The Official Victoria Stilwell Podcast
summary: We’re keeping it light this summer with episodes every other week so you can stay inspired without missing the sunshine. In this episode of the Victoria Stilwell Positively Podcast, Victoria sits down with UK-based trainer, and VSPDT, Adam Daines. Adam shares the story of how a difficult experience with traditional training methods as a 14-year-old shaped his unwavering commitment to positive reinforcement. Now running his successful business, Avon Dog Services, Adam opens up about his work with working-line dogs who often “go self-employed” when their natural needs aren’t being met. From practical enrichment ideas like tracking and “search and retrieve” games to his pioneering Training Holidays, Adam is redefining what pet dog training can look like. Plus, the two discuss their behind-the-scenes experiences on the Dog Academy series—camera interruptions and all! Whether you're a dog guardian struggling with reactivity or a future trainer trying to find your path, this episode is packed with insight, humor, and heart. To learn more about Adam, visit Avon Dog Services.

title: 99 | Lead with Trust, Not Treats
podcast_name: Beyond Obedience | Dog Training, Dog Behaviour Training, Scared Dogs, Reactive Dogs
summary: 🐾 Hey there, Dog Lover, If you’ve ever felt like your relationship with your dog revolves around treats, you’re not alone.The truth is, treats can buy attention... but they can’t build a relationship. In today’s episode, we're diving into what it really means to lead with trust—not treats.We’ll talk about why real leadership isn’t about transactions or constant reinforcement, but about becoming the calm, steady, trustworthy presence your dog can depend on—no cookie needed. 🚫🍪 Hope you enjoy this one!Be sure to share it with your dog-loving friends. 🐾 Cheers!Tracy Ready to ditch the treat pouch and build real trust?➡️ Join me inside the Fearless Framework Program — a relationship-first program and community designed to help fearful, reactive, and misunderstood dogs find confidence naturally.Learn more and join here: www.beyondobedience.ca/fearless 🎶 Music Credit: Shawn Trotter | www.shawntrotter.com

title: Ep. 9. How to Reduce Your Dog’s Barking Before Baby Arrives: Dog and Baby Prep Tips
podcast_name: Dog and Baby Podcast: Dog training tips for expecting parents
summary: Is your dog’s barking already driving you up the wall and baby isn’t even here yet? In this episode, I’m breaking down how to reduce barking before your baby arrives, why it matters for your postpartum sanity, and what you can start doing right now to set your dog (and yourself) up for success. Whether it’s barking at the door, whining for attention, or just non-stop noise we’re tackling it all with realistic, mom-friendly strategies you can implement today. What You’ll Learn: ✔️ Why barking becomes a huge issue postpartum ✔️ What’s actually triggering your dog’s barking ✔️ Simple, low-effort ways to reduce barking during pregnancy ✔️ What not to do (and why yelling “no” doesn’t work) ✔️ How to prep your dog for a peaceful postpartum environment 🔗 Free Resource Mentioned: Grab my FREE Dog + Baby Prep Checklist to help you start creating a calmer, safer, more structured home for your dog before your baby arrives. ➡️ Get it here! ⸻ 📲 Stay Connected: Let’s keep this conversation going! title: The Lie That Shaped Dog Training For Decades: The Truth About Dominance in Dogs + Why It’s Misunderstood
podcast_name: Listen Dog Training: The Walk & Talk
summary: What if everything you were told about being your dog’s ‘alpha’ was built on a misinterpretation of stressed-out wolves in captivity? In this eye-opening episode of The Walk & Talk, I take you on a deep-dive through the shaky science behind dominance theory, the myth of the ‘alpha dog,’ and the long-lasting impact this outdated ideology still has on modern dog training. You'll learn:  🐾 The real story behind those infamous wolf studies  🐾 Why dominance-based training harms trust, learning, and safety  🐾 What leadership should look like in a healthy dog-human relationship  🐾 How to recognise stress-based behaviours and unmet needs in your dog  🐾 The empowering truth: your dog isn’t trying to rule you—they’re trying to connect with you If you’ve ever felt torn between harsh training advice and doing what feels kind and intuitive, this episode will give you the clarity and confidence to lead your dog with compassion and structure. Let’s rewrite the rules—and reclaim the joy. 💡  Want to dive deeper? Explore my digital dog training courses designed to give you the exact tools to build connection, teach essential skills, and support a truly happy life with your dog—without the confusion or conflict.  Browse all courses at www.listendogtraining.com ✨  Free training tips, tutorials & support ⬇️  →  Instagram: @listendogtraining  →  TikTok: @listendogtraining

title: 98 | Your Fearful Dog Doesn't Have to Like You
podcast_name: Beyond Obedience | Dog Training, Dog Behaviour Training, Scared Dogs, Reactive Dogs
summary: 🐾 Hey there, Dog Lover, We all want our dogs to like us—it's only natural.But when you're working with a fearful or reactive dog, likeability isn't the goal. Trust is. In today’s episode, we’re digging into the hidden trap of trying to "win" your dog's affection—and why focusing on being trusted instead of liked changes everything.We'll explore what real leadership looks like (hint: it's not about bribery or constant cheerleading) and how you can start showing up as the steady, calm, trustworthy presence your dog actually needs. And yes... we're having a little fun with a classic "Pick Me, Choose Me" reference from Grey’s Anatomy too. 😉🐾 Hope you enjoy this one!Be sure to share it with your dog-loving friends who might need a little reminder that they’re already enough. 💜 Cheers!  Tracy Ready to take the next step with your fearful dog?➡️ Join me inside the Fearless Framework — a trust-based -relationship-first coaching program and community built specifically for reactive, fearful, and misunderstood dogs.This isn’t just about training drills or obedience commands. It’s about understanding your dog’s mind, building real trust, and walking through the world together with calm confidence.You and your dog deserve that—and I can’t wait to help you get there! 🐾 Learn more: www.beyondobedience.ca/fearless 🎶 Music Credit: Shawn Trotter | www.shawntrotter.com
